message:
refactor(engine): extract normalize_record_id helper in IFC provenance index

The flow event, flow proof, declass receipt, and confinement claim
insertion paths each contained inline empty-ID validation that only
checked `is_empty()` without trimming whitespace. A whitespace-only ID
like " " would pass the check, be stored as a key, and silently corrupt
lookups.

Changes:
- Extract `normalize_record_id(id, record_type)` helper that trims
  whitespace and returns `ProvenanceError::EmptyId` for blank inputs,
  matching the existing `normalize_extension_id()` pattern
- Replace all four inline empty-ID guards with `normalize_record_id()`
  calls that write the trimmed value back into the stored record before
  persisting, so leading/trailing spaces never reach storage
- Add edge-case tests verifying whitespace-only IDs are rejected for
  each record type
- Add integration tests for normalized storage round-trip behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
